commands.repair_reason: No repair lane is needed; maintainer action is to clear or confirm checks and decide whether the supplied Slack proof is enough to merge.; Cleared: No security or supply-chain concern was found; the diff only changes Slack dispatch logic and colocated tests, with no dependency, workflow, secret, or package metadata changes. (sha=05c24ca1515afad88d2c536d8817257416ca2328)
commands.review_summary:
The PR makes Slack `status_final` draft boundaries a no-op and adds regression/sanity tests for assistant and reasoning boundary events.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +77. Total +87 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main calls `forceNewMessage()` from assistant/reasoning boundaries and that helper clears Slack message ids; the inspected before screenshot shows the resulting stack of orphaned progress drafts.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed; maintainer action is to clear or confirm checks and decide whether the supplied Slack proof is enough to merge.

target.title: fix(slack): keep one draft message in progress mode
target.body:
## Summary

In `channels.slack.streaming.mode = "progress"` (internally `status_final`) the Slack draft preview is supposed to be a single rolling message that gets finalised in place by `finalizeSlackPreviewEdit` when the final reply lands. In practice, multi-segment assistant turns (text ŌåÆ tool ŌåÆ text ŌåÆ tool ŌåÆ text) ended up posting **one new draft message per assistant segment**, each individually edited, with the final reply edited into only the *last* one ŌĆö leaving the earlier drafts as orphaned progress messages in the channel.

## Root cause

`onDraftBoundary` is wired to every `onAssistantMessageStart` and `onReasoningEnd` event in `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts`. The boundary unconditionally calls `draftStream.forceNewMessage()` ŌĆö which clears `messageId` so the next `draftStream.update()` falls back to `chat.postMessage` instead of editing the existing preview.

That behaviour is correct for `partial` / `block` modes where each assistant segment is its own delivered chunk. In `status_final` it actively breaks the single-rolling-preview contract.

## Fix

Make `onDraftBoundary` a no-op in `status_final` mode. Other modes are unchanged.

```ts
const onDraftBoundary = !shouldUseDraftStream
  ? undefined
  : async () => {
      if (streamMode === "status_final") {
        return;                          // <-- new
      }
      if (hasStreamedMessage) {
        draftStream?.forceNewMessage();
        ...
      }
      ...
    };
```

## Tests

Two new cases in `dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts`:

1. **Regression**: in progress mode, `forceNewMessage` is **never** called even when `onAssistantMessageStart` / `onReasoningEnd` fire mid-turn.
2. **Sanity**: in `partial`/`replace` mode, `forceNewMessage` is still called on the assistant boundary so we haven't silenced non-progress modes.

All 67 tests in `dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts` + `dispatch.streaming.test.ts` pass.

## How we found it

Production Slack bot was posting multiple progress messages per turn instead of one editable preview. Traced through `pipeline.runtime` / `dispatch.ts` to the unconditional `forceNewMessage()` call on the assistant boundary.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** In `channels.slack.streaming.mode = "progress"` (internally `status_final`), a multi-segment assistant turn (text ŌåÆ tool ŌåÆ text ŌåÆ tool ŌåÆ text) was posting one new draft message per assistant segment instead of editing a single rolling preview in place. The earlier drafts were left as orphaned progress messages above the final reply.

**Real environment tested:** Production Slack workspace, real Slack bot identity connecting via socket mode, OpenClaw agent running the patched bundle on the same EC2 host that previously reproduced the bug. Slack streaming config in use: `channels.slack.streaming = { mode: "progress" }` per account, agent identity emoji as the ack reaction, replies threaded (`replyToMode: "all"`). No other Slack-side config or workspace settings changed between the before/after runs.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. Built the patched extension bundle from this branch and deployed it onto the production agent host (replacing the previously running bundle in place; same gateway, same account credentials, same Slack app).
2. Restarted the agent so the Slack monitor reconnected with the new dispatch wiring.
3. From the same Slack channel that reproduced the original bug, sent the same kind of prompt that triggers a tool-using turn (multiple assistant text segments interleaved with tool calls).
4. Watched the channel from iOS Slack while the turn streamed, then again after the final reply landed.

**Evidence after fix:**

Before ŌĆö same single turn produced a stack of separate progress messages (one per assistant segment between tool calls), each individually edited, with the final reply landing as yet another message:

![before ŌĆö multiple orphaned progress messages](https://github.com/mycarrysun/openclaw/releases/download/slack-progress-proof/slack-progress-before.jpg)

After ŌĆö same agent on the patched bundle, same workflow: one rolling progress draft that gets edited in place and finalised into the answer (highlighted in red). No orphaned progress messages above the final reply:

![after ŌĆö single rolling preview, finalised in place](https://github.com/mycarrysun/openclaw/releases/download/slack-progress-proof/slack-progress-after.jpg)

iOS Slack screenshots, redacted by the workspace owner.

**Observed result after fix:** The Slack channel showed exactly one draft preview message for the whole multi-segment turn. As each assistant text segment streamed, that single message updated in place (`chat.update`) instead of a new one being posted. When the final reply arrived, `finalizeSlackPreviewEdit` edited the same message into the final answer. No orphaned progress messages were left above the final reply. The "after" screenshot is the live channel state, not a staged repro.

**What was not tested:** `partial` and `block`/`replace` streaming modes were not exercised against a live Slack workspace in this run ŌĆö they're covered by the new `dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts` sanity case that asserts `forceNewMessage` is still called on the assistant boundary in those modes, so the no-op is scoped to `status_final` only.
target.author: mycarrysun
